title: Stage 6 sqlmap-candidate export
description: Documentation-only shape reference for the Stage 6 -> sqlmap-wrapper handoff. Not executed or imported by any Python code in this repo -- both sides implement hand-rolled validation (render_sqlmap_json.py writes this shape; sqlmap-wrapper/sqlmap_wrapper/import_candidates.py's validate_candidate() checks it) rather than depending on a JSON Schema validation library, consistent with this project's minimal-dependency convention. Bump the version suffix and add a new sibling file if this shape ever changes -- never edit this file in place.

finding_id.description: bluebird-whitebox findings.finding_id -- not a real cross-DB FK, see sqlmap-wrapper/DATA_DICTIONARY.md

endpoint.description: route path only, e.g. '/signup' -- never a real host

target_param_name.description: null means: no reactive dynamic-probe evidence exists for this finding -- injection point must be determined manually, see `note`

param_defaults.description: sibling request-body values (e.g. signupPOST's username/email/password alongside whichever field is target_param_name), sourced from the same human-supplied request-templates.json Stage 4.5 uses. null if no template was supplied at export time or none matched -- the wrapper must then ask a human before it can build a request body for this candidate.

probe_classifications.description: {probe_name: classification}, only present when target_param_name is non-null

note.description: human-actionable note, set whenever target_param_name is null
